name: DC-15s Sidearm
description: The standard sidearm of the Grand Army of the Republic. Compact, reliable, and built for sustained field use. The dynamic power cell slowly regenerates charge between shots, making it forgiving in prolonged engagements.

trait.name: Dynamic Cell
trait.description: The smart power cell regenerates 2 charges per round the weapon is not fired. Once per scene, when you would run dry, you may instead fire on reserves — treating the weapon as loaded for one final shot.

name: Merr-Sonn IR-5 Intimidator
description: A rapid-fire blaster pistol that fires at the rate of a repeating blaster. Slightly underpowered compared to a standard sidearm, but the integrated targeting computer compensates — keeping bolts on target through sustained automatic fire.

trait.name: Repeating Fire
trait.description: This weapon fires at the rate of a repeating blaster. An integrated targeting computer compensates for the rapid fire rate — Autofire does not impose a Control penalty for lacking a stock or for the weapon's fire rate.

gambits.name: Spray
gambits.rule: Reduce your Effect by 1 Tier. Select two targets in the Zone — the resolved Effect Tier applies to both.

gambits.name: Spray and Pray
gambits.rule: Reduce your Effect by 1 Tier. Saturate an entire Zone in range — the resolved Effect Tier applies to every enemy in it. The weapon then gains [Disabled]; spend a Maneuver to recover (clearing the overheated barrel, spent magazine, and recalibrating the targeting computer).

name: BlasTech DL-18
description: The galaxy's most common sidearm. Cheap, available everywhere, and utterly dependable. Every scoundrel, merchant, and frontier colonist owns one. It won't impress anyone, but it will keep you alive.

name: BlasTech DH-17
description: The favored sidearm of Imperial officers and shipboard security. A built-in fire control package rewards careful aim, and a field-modifiable burst mode can shred targets at close range — once.

trait.name: Fire Control
trait.description: Precision optics and integrated fire control package. Optimizes the Overwatch Maneuver — when you use Overwatch, Step Up your Control Die for that attack.

name: Westar-34
description: A Mandalorian-forged blaster pistol built for close-range lethality. Heavy frame, tight tolerances, exceptional stopping power. Designed to punch through light armor at close range and keep shooting in any environment.

trait.name: Mandalorian Forged
trait.description: Built to Mandalorian standards: immune to malfunctions from environmental conditions (water, sand, vacuum, extreme heat). Does not present an Opening when fired in the Engaged Zone.

name: Relby-k23
description: A heavy bounty hunter's pistol with an extended barrel and integrated targeting scope. Hits harder and reaches farther than any standard sidearm. Popular with professionals who need a single, decisive shot.

trait.name: Low Refraction Bolt
trait.description: Engineered for minimal beam dispersion — the bolt loses almost no cohesion over distance, giving this pistol exceptional range and stopping power.

gambits.name: Excruciating Hit
gambits.rule: Reduce your Effect by 1 Tier. The bolt's unique frequency causes intense, lingering pain in biological targets. Inflict [Disoriented – Lingering] in addition to the resolved damage.

name: Lightsaber
description: An elegant weapon for a more civilized age. A blade of pure, contained plasma requiring intense focus and attunement to wield safely.

trait.name: Cauterize
trait.description: Intense plasma heat instantly sears flesh and melts steel. Cannot inflict [Bleeding]. Automatically Steps Up the final Effect Tier by 1 when cutting through inanimate objects (blast doors, bulkheads, binders).

gambits.name: Plasma Shear
gambits.rule: Reduce your Effect by 1 Tier. Shift your attack target from Defense Resistance to Evasion Resistance — this strike explicitly ignores the [Ironclad] trait, turning a physical block into a test of the target's ability to dodge.

name: Nightsister Energy Bow
description: A handcrafted carbon-alloy bow strung with a plasma coupler — the signature weapon of the Nightsister Hunters of Dathomir. When drawn, the string pulls concentrated plasma from a reservoir in the grip to form a glowing arrow of superheated energy. The bow is silent, terrifyingly accurate, and punches through armor that would shrug off a blaster bolt. When the reservoir runs dry, the plasma string itself becomes a cutting weapon — white-hot and capable of severing limbs at engaged range. The bow folds down for concealment. Possession marks the bearer as either a Dathomiri witch or someone who killed one.

trait.name: Whisper Bow
trait.description: Silent: The bow fires without sound or muzzle flash — attacks do not reveal the wielder's position or break concealment. Concealable: The bow folds to half its length for transport and can be hidden under a cloak or in a standard gear bag. Dual-Mode: When Engaged, the Energy Bow may be used as a Melee weapon — the plasma coupler string cuts and electrocutes. Use Athletics (Physique) for the melee attack; the string deals damage as a Light Melee weapon.

gambits.name: Plasma Pierce
gambits.rule: Reduce your Effect by 1 Tier. This shot ignores armor, forcing the opponent to defend with Reflexes alone. Against NPCs, target Evasion unless a special trait determines otherwise.

name: Bryar Rifle
description: A rugged outer rim blaster rifle built from surplus parts and hard-won experience. Undersells its lethality — the long barrel keeps bolts on target well beyond what its battered frame suggests. A favorite of independent operators who can't afford to be picky.

trait.name: Deceptive Range
trait.description: The long barrel minimizes bolt dispersion over distance. The max range band (zones 31+) imposes only −1 Control step instead of the standard −2.

name: BlasTech E-11
description: The standard-issue blaster rifle of the Imperial Army. Reliable, modular, and available everywhere — including black markets and battlefield salvage. The retractable stock and integrated scope make it effective at range; folded down, it punches through tight corridors like a carbine.

trait.name: Scope & Retractable Stock
trait.description: Two hardware modes in one frame. Scope: Optimizes the Overwatch Maneuver — when you use Overwatch, Step Up your Control Die for that attack. Collapsible Stock: When the stock is folded, this weapon may target the Engaged Zone (Range 0). Firing while Engaged still presents an Opening.

name: BlasTech DC-15 Clone Rifle
description: The assault backbone of the Grand Army of the Republic. Three years after Order 66, these are still turning up on outer rim battlefields — in rebel caches, black market crates, and the hands of veterans who never stopped fighting. Built for sustained automatic fire in any environment.

trait.name: Assault Configuration
trait.description: Engineered from the ground up for sustained automatic fire. Autofire does not impose an additional Control step-down — the weapon's design absorbs the fire rate penalty inherently.

name: BlasTech DLT-19
description: A heavy blaster rifle built for the long reach. Standard kit for Imperial heavy weapons specialists and the weapon of choice for anyone who needs to engage targets the other side of a landing field. Belt-fed and relentless — it does not run dry.

trait.name: Belt-Fed Power Cell
trait.description: This weapon feeds from a continuous power belt rather than a standard pack — it carries 100 charges. Narrative truth: it does not run dry in a firefight.

name: BlasTech EE-3
description: A compact, hard-hitting blaster carbine built for professionals. Short enough to clear a doorway, heavy enough to put down what comes through it. The pistol grip is the tell — this weapon is designed to work in your hands at any distance, no stock required.

trait.name: Pistol Grip
trait.description: Designed to be fired one-handed at close quarters. This weapon may target the Engaged Zone (Range 0). Firing while Engaged still presents an Opening — the grip changes the geometry, not the danger.

name: BlasTech A280
description: The weapon that won more than a few early skirmishes against Imperial armor. A full-power blaster rifle with no frills and no compromises. The damage track says everything worth saying about the A280.

name: Merr-Sonn Q-2
description: The galaxy's most common backup weapon. Cheap, concealable, and found in every boot, waistband, and hidden pocket on the outer rim. No frills. No range to speak of. But at arm's length in a dark corridor, it does exactly what it needs to.

name: Merr-Sonn Happy Surprise
description: Named honestly. Slightly more reach than the Q-2, but the real feature is that nobody expects to find it. Merr-Sonn engineered the frame specifically to defeat casual detection — smooth surfaces, minimal signature, no protruding components. You don't draw this weapon. You reveal it.

trait.name: Concealable Frame
trait.description: Engineered to defeat detection. Very Difficult to find via pat-down or standard weapon scanner — smooth surfaces, no protruding components, minimal energy signature.

name: Merr-Sonn Quickfire-4
description: Maximum lethality in minimum space. The Quickfire-4 hits harder than any holdout has a right to — the trade-off is a range measured in arm-lengths. This is the weapon you reach for when everything else has gone wrong and the threat is close enough to touch.

name: KYD-21
description: Built for operators who can't afford to be heard. The integrated silencer is factory-fitted, not a mod — it buffers both sound and muzzle flash without reducing stopping power. Standard issue for those who consider discretion a tactical requirement.

trait.name: Integrated Silencer
trait.description: This weapon produces no sound when fired. When used from hiding against an unaware target at a range beyond the effective band (Zone 3+), the shooter's position remains undetected after the shot.

name: SoroSuub X-30 Lancer
description: A competition target pistol with a precision-machined barrel that puts sporting blasters to shame at distance. Hits lighter than a military sidearm, but reaches just as far. Favored by hunters, scouts, and anyone who believes the first shot should be the only shot.

trait.name: Precision Target Barrel
trait.description: Machined to competition tolerances for deliberate, accurate fire. Optimizes the Overwatch Maneuver — when you use Overwatch, Step Up your Control Die for that attack.

name: Luxan Penetrator
description: A sporting blaster with no sporting pretensions. The focused beam delivers Medium chassis energy from a pistol frame — but the collimation that makes it hit so hard also makes it unforgiving to aim. There is no spray-and-pray with a Penetrator. Every shot is a commitment.

trait.name: Focused Beam
trait.description: The tightly collimated bolt is difficult to place with precision. Step Down your Control Die on all attacks with this weapon. What lands, lands hard.

name: SoroSuub QuickSnap 36T
description: The outer rim's everyman carbine. Mass-produced, widely distributed, and found in frontier armories, rebel caches, and cantina back rooms across the galaxy. Not glamorous. Not precise at distance. But it's available, it's affordable, and when you need a carbine it's always the QuickSnap that turns up.

trait.name: QuickSnap Action
trait.description: The streamlined action is built for immediate deployment. Drawing this weapon costs no Maneuver.

name: E-5 Droid Blaster
description: Standard-issue for Separatist battle droids during the Clone Wars. The Empire decommissioned millions of them. They went somewhere — and that somewhere was every black market, surplus dealer, and scavenger operation in the outer rim. Cheap, plentiful, and hits harder than it has any right to for the price.

name: Jer'N be' Milad Vel Lightning Gun
description: Not a blaster. Fires compressed packets of charged sub-atomic particles — an elongated bolt of actinic blue-white that arrives with a ripping crack of thunder. Exotic, rare, and unmistakable. Six shots per power pack. You will not go unnoticed.

trait.name: Charged Particle Bolt
trait.description: This weapon fires compressed particle packets, not a coherent energy bolt — it cannot be deflected by a lightsaber or energy-based deflection. The distinctive blue-white bolt and thunder-crack are unmistakable; the shooter's position is always revealed after firing. This weapon carries only 6 shots per power pack.

name: Knife
description: The oldest weapon in the galaxy, present on every world in some form. A simple blade with no electronics to fail and nothing to explain. Nobody looks twice at a belt knife on the outer rim — and at close range it is as lethal as anything more expensive.

trait.name: Concealed Carry
trait.description: A blade this size hides anywhere. Passes casual searches without question. Only a deliberate, thorough search has any chance of finding it.

name: Vibroblade
description: A powered blade with a compact generator driving ultrasonic vibrations along the cutting edge. Those vibrations let it shear through material that would stop a plain blade cold — armor plating, thick hide, equipment straps. Carried by soldiers, bounty hunters, and anyone who understands the gap between a knife and a weapon.

trait.name: Vibro-Edge
trait.description: High-frequency vibrations allow the blade to cut and tear with exceptional efficiency. When you roll 8 or higher on your Control die, inflict [Bleeding] in addition to the resolved Effect.

name: Fighting Staff
description: A two-meter fighting staff — the unassuming weapon of wanderers, monks, and anyone who prefers not to draw attention on the way into a room. Requires no power cells, no maintenance, and no explanation. In trained hands it is a reactive defensive weapon as much as an offensive one.

trait.name: Balanced Guard
trait.description: The staff's two-handed grip and natural balance lend it to deflection and interception. When you take the Defend Maneuver against a melee attack, Step Up your Defense die.

name: Axe
description: A simple, heavy-bladed chopping tool that has served as a weapon since the first sentient being needed to make their point clearly. No power cells, no moving parts beyond the swing. What it lacks in technology it compensates for in mass and geometry. Found everywhere from lumberyards to frontier homesteads.

name: Vibro-ax
description: A broad-bladed axe driven by a compact vibration generator that turns an already brutal weapon into something capable of splitting durasteel. Popular with Gamorrean guards, certain species who favor it culturally, and anyone who wants their statement made with authority. Takes two hands to swing properly and delivers that investment directly to the target.

trait.name: Vibro-Edge
trait.description: High-frequency vibrations allow the blade to cut and tear with exceptional efficiency. When you roll 8 or higher on your Control die, inflict [Bleeding] in addition to the resolved Effect.

gambits.name: Heavy Cleave
gambits.rule: Reduce your Effect by 1 Tier. The resolved Effect applies to one additional target in the Engaged Zone.

name: Duelist Vibrorapier
description: A precision vibro-blade built along a traditional rapier profile — slender, balanced, and engineered for the kind of deliberate bladework that separates a trained duelist from a brawler. The vibration generator is fitted into the guard to keep the blade's center of balance intact. A wealthy choice, and a professional one.

trait.name: Vibro-Edge
trait.description: High-frequency vibrations allow the blade to cut and tear with exceptional efficiency. When you roll 8 or higher on your Control die, inflict [Bleeding] in addition to the resolved Effect.

gambits.name: Precision Thrust
gambits.rule: Reduce your Effect by 1 Tier. Shift the attack target from Defense Resistance to Evasion Resistance — the rapier's narrow profile finds the gap in any guard.

name: Force Pike
description: A two-meter SoroSuub graphite staff tipped with a vibro-edged head and a dual-mode emitter. At the lowest setting it delivers a jolt of pain capable of dropping most beings. At the highest, the vibro-head will cut through durasteel. Standard equipment for the Emperor's Royal Guard and Senate security — a statement of authority and a functional weapon in equal measure.

trait.name: Vibro-Edge
trait.description: High-frequency vibrations allow the blade to cut and tear with exceptional efficiency. When you roll 8 or higher on your Control die, inflict [Bleeding] in addition to the resolved Effect.

gambits.name: Set the Pike
gambits.rule: Reduce your Effect by 1 Tier. Declare a held position — the next enemy that voluntarily enters your Engaged Zone presents an Opening you may Exploit before they can act. This Exploit does not cost your Exploit pip.

name: Combat Gloves
description: Reinforced gloves with hardened plating at the knuckles and palm heel — purpose-built to turn a fist into a weapon without looking like one. Worn under work gloves, dress gloves, or on their own, they draw no attention in most social settings. What lands, lands harder than it should.

trait.name: Concealed Weapon
trait.description: Resembles ordinary gloves under casual inspection. Passes most social-context weapon checks without question. When worn, unarmed strikes are treated as weapon attacks for the purpose of overcoming resistances.

name: Snap Baton
description: A Merr-Sonn collapsible baton: 12 centimeters folded, one full meter deployed. The exterior is wrapped in dark sound-absorbing material that muffles the snap of deployment. Popular in urban environments with thugs, enforcers, and people who appreciate having a weapon that fits in a coat pocket until it is needed.

trait.name: Collapsible
trait.description: Folds to 12cm — fits in a pocket or clips to a utility belt unnoticed. Sound-absorbing material dampens the deployment snap. Very Difficult to detect in a casual search.

name: DDC Defender
description: A no-frills personal defense blaster, legally available without restriction on most worlds. It won't win any awards for accuracy or range, but a DDC Defender in your belt beats an empty holster — and nobody questions why you have one.

name: Merr-Sonn Quick-Six
description: The most common legal sidearm in the outer rim. Merr-Sonn's Model 6 was designed with one goal: maximum availability. Sold at every general store, trading post, and supply depot without question or documentation. The frame is balanced for a fast draw, the action is smooth, and it hits slightly harder than most sporting blasters its size. If someone on the frontier has a blaster, odds are it's a Quick-Six.

name: Fists
description: Bare-knuckle strikes, headbutts, and grapples. Every sentient being can throw a punch — most just wish they were better at it.

trait.name: Desperate Measure
trait.description: Bare fists cannot be disarmed, confiscated, or depleted. All damage is stun only — even at full force, an unarmed strike cannot kill. It can only stagger, disorient, or knock unconscious.

name: Cathar Claws
description: Retractable keratin claws honed by generations of predatory evolution. Cathar claws are weapons of last resort and cultural pride — drawn when words and blasters have failed.

trait.name: Natural Weapons
trait.description: Retractable claws are part of your body — they cannot be disarmed, confiscated, or depleted. Lethal by default; switch to stun mode to use the unarmed chassis stun values (0/1/2) instead.

name: Micro Blaster
description: A blaster pistol barely larger than a comlink. Two shots, no sights, no accuracy beyond arm's reach. It exists for one purpose: the weapon nobody knows you have. Diplomats tape them to their thighs. Smugglers tuck them inside boot heels. The first shot is a surprise. There usually isn't a need for a third.

trait.name: Deep Conceal
trait.description: Small enough to evade standard pat-downs and weapon scanners. Detection requires a dedicated search specifically looking for holdouts. Effective range is extremely short — same zone only. Clip holds 2 shots.

name: Imperial Munitions KK-5
description: A compact military-grade blaster pistol manufactured by Imperial Munitions for internal security forces and ISB field agents. Never sold on the civilian market. Every one on the street was stolen, stripped from a dead officer, or quietly diverted from a supply depot. Reliable, accurate, and the kind of weapon that gets you executed for possessing.

trait.name: Military Grade
trait.description: Built to Imperial military specification. No stun setting — designed to kill, not incapacitate. Reliable action; immune to jam and misfire effects that plague cheaper black market blasters.

name: BlasTech T-6 "Thunderer"
description: A snub-nosed heavy blaster pistol with a massively oversized power cell and reinforced barrel housing. The T-6 hits like a carbine packed into a pistol frame. The recoil is punishing and the effective range is short — but within that range, nothing short of vehicle armor stops a Thunderer bolt. Popular with pirates, enforcers, and anyone who needs one shot to end a conversation.

trait.name: Snub Barrel
trait.description: Maximum effective range is limited to short — attacks beyond medium range are not possible. Within same zone, the concentrated bolt resolves at +1 Effect tier.

name: Merr-Sonn Model 434 "DeathHammer"
description: The Model 434 is the most popular aftermarket heavy pistol in the Outer Rim. More powerful than a standard sidearm, compact enough to holster, and available from any arms dealer with connections. It hits hard and has a stun setting, which is why bounty hunters adopted it as a standard tool. Merr-Sonn sells millions of them. The Empire restricts them. Nobody cares.

trait.name: Workhorse
trait.description: A reliable, well-built medium blaster pistol with a stun setting. No mechanical surprises — it works every time you pull the trigger.

name: Morellian Enforcer Pistol
description: A slugthrower hand-cannon firing solid metal projectiles at hypersonic velocity. No power pack, no blaster gas — just machined ammunition and a magnetic accelerator barrel. The Enforcer exists because some targets have energy-deflecting shields, cortosis weave, or lightsabers. A solid slug doesn't care about any of that. The report is deafening. The recoil will break your wrist if you're not braced.

trait.name: Solid Slug
trait.description: Deals Projectile damage. The slug is a physical mass projectile — unaffected by energy shields, cortosis weave, and personal deflector fields. Any defense or ability that specifically blocks or deflects energy weapons has no effect against the Enforcer. Firing eliminates any possibility of stealth.

name: Tenloss DX-2 Disruptor Pistol
description: A sleek pistol-frame disruptor that fires a focused molecular disruption beam. The bolt doesn't burn or pierce — it unravels molecular bonds on contact. Flesh, armor, durasteel — everything comes apart the same way. Possession is a capital offense in every system in the galaxy. The Tenloss Syndicate manufactures them anyway. Short range, total lethality.

trait.name: Disintegration
trait.description: If a target is reduced to 0 Vitality by this weapon, they are disintegrated — no incapacitation, no stabilization, no recovery. Maximum effective range is short — attacks beyond medium range are not possible.

name: Tenloss DXR-6 Disruptor Rifle
description: The rifle-frame variant of the Tenloss disruptor platform. A longer barrel and enhanced focusing array extend the disruption beam to standard rifle range — still short compared to a blaster rifle, but far enough to make this an assassination weapon rather than a brawling tool. Two-handed, conspicuous, and impossible to explain away. If you're carrying a DXR-6, you've already decided how the conversation is going to end.

trait.name: Disintegration
trait.description: If a target is reduced to 0 Vitality by this weapon, they are disintegrated — no incapacitation, no stabilization, no recovery. Two-handed. Range extends to medium — longer than the pistol variant but still short for a rifle.

name: Saberdart Launcher
description: A compact wrist-mounted pneumatic launcher that fires slim micro-darts at short range. The launcher itself is harmless — a precision delivery system for whatever substance is loaded into the dart. Kamino assassins made the design infamous. The launcher is legal in most systems. The payloads generally are not.

trait.name: Delivery Device
trait.description: The saberdart launcher deals no damage on its own. On a successful hit, the loaded substance is delivered to the target. Effects are determined by the payload, not the weapon. Darts are consumed on use. Concealed while worn; detection requires a deliberate search.

name: MM9 Wrist Rocket System
description: A forearm-mounted micro-rocket launcher originally designed for Mandalorian combat armor integration. Three miniature concussion rockets loaded in a rotating magazine — point your fist and fire. The rockets are unguided but fast. At close range, they don't need guidance. The wrist mount leaves both hands free, which is the real value — you can fire rockets while holding a blaster, a vibroblade, or someone's collar.

trait.name: Wrist Mounted
trait.description: Concealed while worn under armor or heavy clothing. Leaves both hands free — firing does not require drawing or holstering. Magazine holds 3 rockets. Rockets are consumable.

gambits.name: Concussive Splash
gambits.description: Reduce your Effect by 1 Tier. The rocket detonation catches one additional target adjacent to the primary target. The reduced Effect tier applies to the secondary target.

name: Wrist Flame Projector
description: A compact fuel reservoir and igniter assembly mounted to the forearm, concealed beneath a sleeve or gauntlet. Squeeze the trigger plate in your palm and a three-meter cone of liquid flame erupts from the wrist nozzle. Mandalorian warriors made these famous. Everyone else uses them because fire solves problems that blasters can't.

trait.name: Liquid Fire
trait.description: Deals Fire damage. The target defends with Evasion — fire cannot be Endured without sealed armor, an energy barrier, or equivalent protection. Concealed while worn; detection requires a deliberate search. Fuel canister holds 3 bursts before requiring replacement.

gambits.name: Set Ablaze
gambits.description: Reduce your Effect by 1 Tier. The target gains [Hazard 1] — Lingering (burning). 1 Vitality per round until extinguished (requires an action or assistance).

name: Duo-Flechette Rifle
description: A short-barreled scattergun that fires twin bursts of razor-sharp durasteel flechettes in a wide cone. Each pull of the trigger sends hundreds of needle-thin shards downrange — they shred fabric, flesh, and light armor at close quarters. Beyond medium range the spread disperses too much to be effective. Up close, it's the most brutal anti-personnel weapon in the underworld.

trait.name: Shredder
trait.description: Deals Projectile damage. Attacks within the same zone resolve at +1 Effect tier. Attacks beyond medium range are not possible — the spread disperses completely.

gambits.name: Scatter Burst
gambits.description: Reduce your Effect by 1 Tier. The flechette cone hits wide — select two targets in the same zone. The resolved Effect tier applies to both.
